retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Initial And Symptom-Oriented Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: When a patient with confirmed AIP presents with an acute attack, the usual first approach is to load the patient with a high carbohydrate diet or intravenously administered dextrose to inhibit hepatic ALAS1 transcription. Administration of 10% dextrose in 0.45% saline should start immediately. If the patient does not present with weakness, vomiting, or hyponatremia, a trial of a high carbohydrate diet for 48 hours before starting specific treatment is the current recommendation. [20] [13] [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: AIP is a rare autosomal dominant condition that runs a chronic course with recurrent acute episodes of neurovisceral symptoms. Trials are giving hope to find a definitive cure to this life -long disease. Once the diagnosis of AIP or some other acute hepatic porphyria is confirmed, patients should be thoroughly counseled and provided with the list of trigger factors (that can precipitate an attack) for avoidance, especially drugs that are safe and unsafe. They should know that treatment is only available for acute attacks and that the best way to remain asymptomatic is by preventing precipitants. If they have abdominal pain should go immediately to the emergency department. They should receive education on prognosis and complications and are strongly encouraged to obtain genetic testing done for their offspring.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the etiology of acute intermittent porphyria. Review the evaluation of acute intermittent porphyria. Outline the management approaches and therapeutic options available for acute intermittent porphyria. Describe interprofessional team strategies for improving care coordination and communication to advance early diagnosis and management of acute intermittent porphyria and improve outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Prognosis is good if acute intermittent porphyria is recognized early and treated at the time. The mortality has decreased through the past decades to 5% to 20% (on acute attacks) thanks to new methods of diagnosis and treatment. But if the disease is resistant to heme therapy and is recurrent, the only currently approved way to reduce mortality is with an orthotopic liver transplant. [17] [2] [1] With ongoing trials exploring the efficacy and safety of ERT and liver gene therapy, the prognosis of AIP is expected to become better very soon.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Potential/Experimental Therapies For AIP
retrieved_docs.content: Liver Gene Therapy - Clinical trials using two strategies, HMBS-gene therapy and interference RNA for ALAS1 gene inhibition, are being conducted in patients with AIP. The two strategies include  - the delivery of the HMBS gene to the hepatocytes using a viral vector. The other option is a small interfering RNA (siRNA) directed against aminolevulinic acid synthase, with the objective of reducing delta ALA production. Both of them are still in the trial phase and await approval, pending larger trials that would hopefully provide consistent efficacy and safety. [20] [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Risks Associated With Recurrent IHT
retrieved_docs.content: Some studies are looking for other alternatives. These are in different phases of clinical trials, and this CME chapter at present, only seeks to inform the readers of these potential and futuristic therapies:

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Complications of acute intermittent porphyria (vide infra); arterial hypertension, chronic kidney disease, neurologic deficits, and risk of hepatocellular carcinoma must remain in check by ensuring close observation and review of the patient.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Specific Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Excepting occasional complaints of headache or pyrexia, IHT is well tolerated. However, there are inherent risks associated with recurrent treatments with IHT, of which every caregiver should be cognizant.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Initial And Symptom-Oriented Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Owing to the simulation of symptoms of acute intermittent porphyria by several abdominal, metabolic, and neuropsychiatric conditions, establishing a confirmed diagnosis forms the core of the management of AIP. Avoidance of precipitants, especially drugs, requires extreme emphasis, ingraining it in the patient and relatives' psyche. ( vide infra )

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Acute intermittent porphyria is a rare autosomal dominant disease characterized by a deficiency of hydroxymethylbilane synthase (HMBS). It presents with abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, peripheral neuropathy, and seizures. Treatment for acute attacks is intravenous heme. Definitive treatment is an orthotopic liver transplant. This activity gives an overview of the etiology, clinical presentation, evaluation, management, and treatment of the disease by an interprofessional team.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Hepatic Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Treatment of Acute Attack
retrieved_docs.content: Nutrition and hydration: Carbohydrate loading should be used to correct the fasting state and as a temporizing measure while waiting for an available dose of intravenous hemin. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Potential/Experimental Therapies For AIP
retrieved_docs.content: Enzyme Replacement Therapy [ERT] - Based on the experience of administering doses of recombinant human HMBS/PBGD (rhPBGD) protein in a mouse model of AIP that reduced plasma PBG accumulation during an acute attack induced after phenobarbital challenge, in 2002 the European Medicines Agency (EMA) granted recombinant human HMBS/PBGD an orphan designation (EU/3/ 02/103). Researchers conducted clinical trials in healthy subjects, asymptomatic HMBS-deficient subjects with increased porphyrin precursor excretion, and AIP patients with repeated attacks. [28] [29] Although the enzyme was able to detoxify PBG metabolites, the treatment approach limitations included its short half-life in circulation and the lack of liver targeting.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Treatment / Management -- Risks Associated With Recurrent IHT
retrieved_docs.content: Currently, the only established cure for acute intermittent porphyria is orthotopic liver transplantation (OLT) with a reported survival rate of around 80%. [26] [27] However, a high risk (40%) of hepatic artery thrombosis with OLT prompted the same authors to recommend reserving the procedure for patients with severe recurrent acute attacks and highly impaired quality of life (QoL).

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Hepatic Porphyria -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Hepatic porphyrias are genetic disorders caused by the defect in an enzyme involved in heme synthesis and accumulation of heme precursors. Hepatic porphyrias include acute intermittent porphyria, variegate porphyria, aminolevulinic acid dehydratase deficiency porphyria, hereditary coproporphyria, and porphyria cutanea tarda. The acute hepatic porphyrias are manifest as neurological attacks (seizures, psychosis, severe abdominal and back pain, and acute polyneuropathy) and, to a lesser extent, with cutaneous manifestations such as photosensitive blistering rash or hypertrichosis. This activity reviews the pathophysiology, evaluation, and management of acute hepatic porphyrias and highlights the role of the interprofessional team in caring for patients affected by these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Acute Intermittent Porphyria -- Evaluation -- Differentiating Between Acute Porphyrias
retrieved_docs.content: As mentioned below (vide infra), most acute porphyrias' symptomatology shows significant overlap. Symptoms of AIP are often clinically indistinguishable from those of hereditary coproporphyria and variegate porphyria. Although the diagnostic approach to distinguish AIP from other acute porphyrias has little evidence backing at present, new evidence-based diagnostic strategies are under development for these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1326
retrieved_docs.content: Acitretin, a systemic retinoid medication, is contraindicated in patients who have a liver disorder (اِضطرَاب الكَبِد), as the drug can cause hepatotoxic effects and exacerbate hepatic dysfunction; therefore, clinicians should avoid prescribing acitretin to individuals with existing liver disease to ensure safe patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 1759
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subnitrate and bismuth subcitrate are both therapeutic agents that may be used to treat الإِسْهال (diarrhea) and اضطراب معدي (gastrointestinal disorder). Each of these bismuth compounds shares the same indicated uses, suggesting they have comparable efficacy in managing diarrheal conditions and related gastrointestinal disturbances.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1437
retrieved_docs.content: Acute posthemorrhagic anemia is a specific subtype of فقر الدم (anemia), indicating that it falls under the broader category of anemia. In clinical practice, فقر الدم is commonly associated with الشعور بالضعف (a feeling of weakness), reflecting the typical symptomatology patients experience when suffering from any form of anemia, including acute posthemorrhagic anemia.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 423
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subnitrate and bismuth subcitrate are therapeutic agents that may be used to treat both الإسهال (diarrhea) and اضطراب معيدي (gastrointestinal disorder). In addition, isopropamide iodide is employed in the management of الإسهال (diarrhea). Together, these medications provide options for alleviating acute and chronic disturbances of the gastrointestinal tract, highlighting their relevance in the treatment of diarrheal illnesses and broader gastrointestinal dysfunction.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2029
retrieved_docs.content: ANTIANEMIC PREPARATIONS are classified as vitamin B12 and folic‑acid supplements; they contain both vitamin B12 and folic acid and are used to treat or prevent anemia that results from deficiencies of these essential nutrients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 4 -- Community 2869
retrieved_docs.content: الشعور بالضعف is clinically associated with فقر الدم, indicating that patients with anemia commonly experience a sensation of weakness.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1535
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subnitrate is a therapeutic agent that may be used to treat اضطراب معديّ (gastrointestinal disorder), indicating its potential role in managing conditions affecting the digestive tract.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 4 -- Community 2928
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subcitrate may be used to treat the gastrointestinal disorder (اضْطِراب مَعِدِيّ).

retrieved_docs.content: entities illustrate the diverse therapeutic roles of magnesium salts, iron compounds and fibrates, while emphasizing common safety considerations—particularly the avoidance of magnesium‑based agents and fibrates in patients with renal impairment and the avoidance of fibrates in hepatic steatosis.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2293
retrieved_docs.content: Transitory is described as a temporary medical condition whose clinical course is characterized by غشي, indicating that episodes of drowsiness or altered consciousness are a typical feature of its progression. This relationship underscores that patients experiencing a transitory condition may present with غشي as part of the condition’s natural course.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2039
retrieved_docs.content: Iron in other combinations is classified as an iron antianemic preparation. Iron antianemic preparations are therapeutic agents used to replenish iron stores and treat iron‑deficiency anemia. Formulations described as “iron in other combinations” contain iron together with additional compounds or nutrients, enhancing absorption or providing complementary benefits while still serving the primary purpose of correcting iron deficiency. Recognizing these combination products as a subset of iron antianemic preparations helps clinicians select appropriate therapy for patients with anemia, especially when co‑existing deficiencies or specific tolerability issues are present.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 620
retrieved_docs.content: overlapping nature of toxicological classifications and the importance of recognizing both acute and delayed effects, as well as intentional versus unintentional exposure, in clinical assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 2 -- Community 1071
retrieved_docs.content: is likewise characterized by a Sudden onset (contextual qualifier) (qualifier value) clinical course, illustrating that abrupt presentation can occur across diverse organ systems. Overall, hemodialysis is a central element of renal therapy that is interconnected with a spectrum of acute disorders, early‑use syndromes, chronic comorbid conditions, and other sudden‑onset pathologies, all of which are important considerations for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 4 -- Community 2900
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is recognized as an etiological descriptor for several medical conditions, including iron‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الحديد), transfusion reaction (تفاعل نقل الدم), mortality (التموت), and acute yellow liver congestion (ضُمورُ الكَبِد الأصفر الحادّ). By serving as an allowed etiological qualifier, it helps clinicians attribute these conditions to specific underlying causes, thereby supporting accurate diagnosis and appropriate patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1387
retrieved_docs.content: Papaverine and its derivatives that are employed in the treatment of functional gastrointestinal disorders are classified as drugs for functional gastrointestinal disorders and are members of the papaverin group.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2299
retrieved_docs.content: Bismuth subnitrate and bismuth subcitrate are both used therapeutically to manage diarrhea (الإِسْهال) and broader gastrointestinal disturbances (اضْطِراب مَعِدِيّ). In contrast, magnesium hydroxide (hydroxid hořečnatý) can provoke diarrhea as an adverse effect. Thus, while magnesium hydroxide may induce diarrheal symptoms, the bismuth compounds serve as treatments for those same symptoms and for related gastrointestinal disorders, highlighting their complementary roles in patient care.
